label: Ninth Hour (Nona)
description: The Byzantine Ninth Hour is sung at the ninth hour of the day (approximately 3:00 PM). It consists of fixed psalmody (Psalms 83, 84, 85 LXX), the Trisagion prayer sequence, a troparion of the day, the Theotokion of the hour, and the Prayer of the Ninth Hour.

sections.label: Opening
sections.notes: The Ninth Hour opens with the Usual Beginning, as do all Byzantine offices.

items.text: The Priest blesses: Blessed is our God, always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

items.label: The Usual Beginning
items.note: Trisagion prayers sequence. Resolved from ninth-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

sections.label: Fixed Psalmody
sections.notes: The Ninth Hour appoints three fixed psalms: 83, 84, and 85 (LXX). These are invariable on ordinary days.

items.label: Psalm 83 (LXX) — How beloved are Thy dwellings, O Lord of hosts
Psalm 84 (LXX) — O Lord, Thou hast been gracious unto Thy land
Psalm 85 (LXX) — Bow down Thine ear, O Lord, and hear me

sections.label: Trisagion Prayers
sections.notes: The Trisagion prayer sequence precedes the troparia.

sections.label: Troparia
sections.notes: The dismissal troparion of the day is sung. The Theotokion of the Ninth Hour follows.

items.label: Troparion of the Day
items.note: Variable troparion: resolved via Menaion resolver or weekday-theme rubric by HorologionEngine. Same infrastructure as Vespers, First Hour, Third Hour, and Sixth Hour troparion slot.

items.label: Theotokion of the Ninth Hour
items.note: Variable: tone-dependent Theotokion from the Octoechos Ninth Hour cycle. Resolved by HorologionEngine as explicit rubric stub at baseline; full text deferred pending Ninth Hour Octoechos data.

sections.label: Prayer of the Ninth Hour and Dismissal
sections.notes: The Prayer of the Ninth Hour concludes the fixed psalmody cycle. The office ends with the standard dismissal.

items.label: Prayer of the Ninth Hour
items.note: Fixed invariable prayer. Resolved from ninth-hour-fixed.json by HorologionEngine.

items.text:
Priest: Christ our true God, through the intercessions of His all-pure Mother, of all the Saints, have mercy on us and save us, as He is good and the Lover of mankind.
Choir: Amen.
